Private Owner Briefing

Prepared for Mat Ishbia

Issue 10 / Vegas Final Report / July 20, 2026

Suns Summer League Pulse

What Phoenix learned in Vegas.

Five games. A winning record. A clearer view of the young group.

WPOR81-79
LNO75-81
WMIL95-88
WDET100-88
LSA81-86

The Read / AVC Interpretation

Phoenix leaves Vegas with more clarity than it had before the event.

The record was positive. The more important result was a four-game frontcourt sample led by Khaman Maluach, plus a final game that widened the evaluation. Phoenix found several reasons to keep watching this group.

01

Maluach set the standard.

His impact held across every appearance. He changed games with his size while expanding the range of shots he could make.

02

The young frontcourt fit together.

Peat brought strength and creation; Fleming supplied shooting and role discipline. Their games complemented Maluach instead of asking each player to do the same work.

03

The final game added names to the file.

Askew, Cooke and Camper all responded to larger roles. The fifth game expanded the useful sample.

Record3-2Five games
Points432Phoenix total
Margin+10Across five games
  • WinPortland81-79
  • LossNew Orleans75-81
  • WinMilwaukee95-88
  • WinDetroit100-88
  • LossSan Antonio81-86

Official NBA game books and box scores. Four games per player.

Khaman Maluach19.5 points, 12.8 rebounds and 2.0 blocks per game. Four straight double-doubles.
19.5 / 12.8
Koa Peat16.8 points, 4.8 rebounds and 2.3 assists per game. Double figures every night.
16.8 PPG
Rasheer Fleming12.0 points and 6.5 rebounds per game. Eight made threes at 42.1%.
42.1% 3PT

Devin Askew

20 points and four steals against San Antonio. He scored 13 points in the fourth quarter.

Javonte Cooke

He bookended the summer with 21 points in the opener and 17 in the finale. Both came off larger offensive opportunities.

Corey Camper Jr.

13 points, eight rebounds and four assists in the finale. He also had two steals and no turnovers.

Phoenix-Area Coverage

Arizona Sports / John GambadoroJul 16 ↗

Phoenix had seen enough from its leading trio.

Gambadoro called the four games with Maluach, Peat and Fleming “highly successful” when he reported that the Suns would shut them down.

Bright Side / John VoitaJul 16 ↗

Fleming's defense may have been his most useful progress.

Voita emphasized improved awareness, footwork and role discipline, arguing that those gains mattered more than one scoring night.

Burn City / Wendy LopezJul 15 ↗

Maluach's approach stood out as much as his production.

Lopez centered his focus on effort, energy, communication and leadership, rather than accepting the idea that he was too good for Summer League.

National Coverage

The Ringer / Danny ChauJul 14 ↗

The three-point shot changed the Maluach conversation.

Chau called the Vegas performance a “massive green flag” and focused on the value of a center who can roll, pop, rebound and protect the rim.

Bleacher Report / Zach BuckleyJul 18 ↗

Maluach made B/R's unofficial first team.

Buckley pointed to the size of his year-over-year growth and the combination of rebounding, shot-blocking and floor spacing.

NBA.com / Shaun PowellJul 14 ↗

Peat earned his own place in the national discussion.

Powell selected Peat as a Day 5 standout and framed his play as an answer to falling to the final pick of the first round.

Six attributed items from accessible Phoenix-area and national coverage through July 19. This is a selected coverage read, not a claim of full media consensus. No verified ESPN article or broadcast transcript was located.

Complete

The Suns have no additional Summer League game scheduled.

The official team schedule shows the five-game Vegas run as Phoenix's final listed basketball activity.

Next phase

Training camp is the next major team evaluation.

The NBA and Suns have not published a Phoenix training-camp date or a 2026-27 Suns preseason game at the verification cutoff.

Calendar

The league calendar is still incomplete.

The NBA has published selected 2026-27 dates, but Phoenix's next game has not been announced.

Pool standings

Phoenix finished in the 3-1 pack.

Nine teams went 3-1 in pool play. Average point differential determined three semifinal places behind the 4-0 Lakers.

Western bracket

Every semifinalist came from the Western Conference.

Memphis beat Houston 101-90. Golden State beat the Lakers 92-88 to set the championship matchup.

Darryn Peterson / UTA

The No. 2 pick arrived as a lead guard.

Peterson averaged 26.5 points and 7.0 assists in two Salt Lake City games, then scored 24 in his Vegas opener.

Cam Boozer / MEM

Memphis found production across the bracket.

Boozer had 24 points, seven rebounds and three assists in the playoff-clinching win, then 12 points, 12 rebounds and five assists in the semifinal.

Yaxel Lendeborg / GSW

Golden State's lottery pick delivered in the semifinal.

Lendeborg had 15 points, four rebounds, three assists and two blocks in the 92-88 win over the Lakers.

Keaton Wagler / LAC

The No. 5 pick finished with his best scoring game.

Wagler scored 26 as the Clippers set a Summer League scoring record in a 128-120 overtime win.

Darius Acuff Jr. / SAC

The No. 7 pick created offense with the ball.

Acuff had 19 points and seven assists in a Vegas win over the Clippers, then scored 26 against Brooklyn.

Carter Bryant / SAS

A second-year wing remains relevant inside the division.

Bryant scored 19 points on 7-for-10 shooting in San Antonio's 70-49 win over New York.

National note

No. 1 pick AJ Dybantsa delivered immediately for Washington.

Dybantsa averaged 25.0 points, 7.0 rebounds and 2.0 assists across two appearances.

Selected official performances, not a final awards ballot or full statistical leaderboard. At cutoff, the title game and official awards remained unresolved.

  • NBA official 2026-27 key dates↗

Draft verified July 19, 2026, at 7:39 PM Phoenix time. Official NBA scorer reports and box scores control the Phoenix results and statistics. The championship was still in progress at the cutoff; no result or award has been claimed. AVC Interpretation is analysis based on public information.

Bottom Line

The next test is whether the same impact holds up with fewer touches and stronger competition.
Produced by

Prepared exclusively for Mat Ishbia. Public information. Owner-level clarity.

Accuracy over volume. If a fact cannot be verified, omit it.
